module.title: Week 18: Log Analysis & SIEM Queries
module.focus: Syslog, Windows Events, Apache logs, SPL

topics.title: Day 1: Syslog Analysis
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Syslog Analysis is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Parse
- Analyze syslog messages
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Parse syslog messages and identify facility, severity, and key data fields
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the format of a syslog message?
topics.interviewAnswer: RFC 5424 format: priority (facility*8+severity), timestamp, hostname, app name, process ID, message ID, structured data, and message body. Facilities include auth, daemon, kern. Severities range from 0 (Emergency) to 7 (Debug).

quiz.question: What RFC defines syslog format?
What severity is Emergency?
What is the facility field?
options: Source type of the log (auth, kern, etc.)

quiz.question: How is priority calculated?
What did the IR team detect by correlating syslog?
options: Lateral movement via SSH session timing

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 1: Syslog Analysis?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Syslog Analysis is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Syslog Analysis?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Syslog Analysis typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Syslog Analysis apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Syslog Analysis concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Syslog Analysis?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Syslog Analysis at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Syslog Analysis?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Syslog Analysis?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Syslog Analysis is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Syslog Analysis during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Syslog Analysis during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Syslog Analysis?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Syslog Analysis can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Syslog Analysis helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Syslog Analysis prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Syslog Analysis relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Syslog Analysis can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Windows Event Log Analysis
topics.description: Master Windows Event Logs and Sysmon
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Windows Event Log Analysis is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Master Windows Event Logs
- Sysmon
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Query Windows Event Logs for security-relevant events
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the most important Windows Event IDs for security?
topics.interviewAnswer: 4624 (successful logon), 4625 (failed logon), 4688 (process creation), 4663 (file access), 7045 (service installed). Sysmon adds IDs for process creation (1), network connections (3), file creation (11), and registry modification (13).

quiz.question: What is Event ID 4624?
What is Event ID 4625?
What does Sysmon add?
What views Windows event logs?
What did analysts trace in the Twitter hack?

topics.title: Day 3: Apache & Web Server Log Analysis
topics.description: Analyze web server logs for attacks
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Apache & Web Server Log Analysis is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Analyze web server logs for attacks
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Identify SQL injection, path traversal, and XSS in Apache logs
topics.interviewQuestion: What indicators of attack appear in Apache access logs?
topics.interviewAnswer: Many 4xx/5xx codes from a single IP, unusual HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE), long query strings, SQL keywords (SELECT, UNION, DROP), and directory traversal sequences (../). Combined Log Format adds Referer and User-Agent fields.

quiz.question: What does Common Log Format include?
What SQL keyword indicates SQL injection?
What does directory traversal use?
What indicates scanning?
options: Many 4xx/5xx codes from a single IP

quiz.question: What was detected in the WordPress campaign?
options: Mass SQL injection with UNION SELECT

topics.title: Day 4: SIEM Search Queries & SPL
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: SIEM Search Queries & SPL is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Write advanced SIEM queries
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Write SPL queries for threat detection use cases
topics.interviewQuestion: What is SPL syntax and key commands?
topics.interviewAnswer: SPL uses pipe syntax: index=main EventCode=4625 | stats count by Account_Name | sort - count. Key commands: search, stats, top, rare, eval, rex, table, timechart, transaction, subsearch, and lookup.

quiz.question: What character pipes commands in SPL?
What command groups and counts events?
What does a subsearch do?
options: Inner query that runs first, passes results to outer

quiz.question: What enriches data with external sources?
What query identified C2 beaconing?

topics.title: Day 5: Log Correlation & Detection Use Cases
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Log Correlation & Detection Use Cases is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Build multi-source correlation rules
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a correlation rule for ransomware detection across log sources
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the types of correlation in SIEM?
topics.interviewAnswer: Aggregation (count events over time), threshold (alert above N count), temporal (sequence of event types), and statistical (baseline deviation). Each maps to different attack patterns.

quiz.question: What correlation type counts events over time?
What correlation uses event sequences?
What indicates password spray success?
options: Failed logins followed by success from same IP

quiz.question: What should detection rules map to?
What correlation caught SQL injection exploitation?
Which best describes Day 5: Log Correlation & Detection Use Cases?
quiz.explanation: Day 5: Log Correlation & Detection Use Cases is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.
